selectivity_cascade._note: MSP-A 200A main / TSP-A 100A 2-pole tenant feeder = 2.0:1 ratio. Below the 3:1 typical instantaneous-selectivity threshold but acceptable for general retail per NEC 240.86 series-rated combinations — full selective coordination is mandated only for elevators (NEC 620.62), emergency systems (NEC 700.27/701.27), and healthcare critical branches (NEC 517.17). Manufacturer series-rating table consultation recommended for AIC stacking.

MSP-A 200A main / TSP-B 100A 2-pole tenant feeder = 2.0:1 ratio. Same rationale as T01. Note: TSP-B C03/C04 (food-service kitchen equipment) use Type D curves for inrush — handled at the TSP-B device tier, no impact on cascade.

MSP-A 200A main / CA-P 60A 2-pole feeder = 3.33:1 ratio, above the 3:1 typical selectivity threshold. CA-P carries fire-pump branch (C05) — see compliance flag for NEC 695.4(A) independent-source requirement.

spd_assessment.location_basis: commercial retail strip mall with service-entry SPD location (NEC 285.23 Type 1 service-entry) plus branch-panel protection (Type 2 at sub-panels). Combined Type 1+2 assembly at MSP-A intake covers direct-strike (Type 1, 10/350µs) and induced-transient (Type 2, 8/20µs) waveforms; Type 2 SPDs deployed at TSP-A, TSP-B, CA-P provide branch-panel residual surge protection per NEC 285.24.

non_compliance_flags.message: CA-P common-area panel carries fire-pump branch circuit (C05 60A Type D, 11.0 kW motor load). NEC 695.4(A) requires fire-pump from an independent service or dedicated tap ahead of the service-disconnecting means — fire-pump CANNOT share a panel with non-essential common-area loads (parking-lot lighting, signage, etc.) because a fault elsewhere on CA-P could open the upstream T03 feeder and disable the fire-pump during a fire event. This example documents a deliberate teaching scenario showing how the SLD surfaces multi-board compliance tensions rather than silently engineering-correcting at the IR tier. Remediation: relocate C05 fire-pump branch to a dedicated 'FP-1' fire-pump controller fed directly from MSP-A line-side (ahead of MSP-A main disconnect) or from an independent service drop per NEC 695.3.

assumptions: Diversity factor 0.85 applied across sub-panel summed loads. Aggregate Imax_total = (9.9 + 16.6 + 19.6 kW) × 0.85 / (√3 × 208V × 0.85) ≈ 127A. Inline LLM derivation; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3 to calc.sld_system_metrics.

Building service brief specifies 208Y/120V 3-phase 4-wire. The upstream MSP-A db-layout intent at electrical/db-layout/examples/us-strip-mall-panelboard/intent-out.json records voltage_v: 240 and phase_arrangement: 'single_phase_split' — this reflects an earlier-draft single-phase MSP authoring snapshot. The downstream sub-panels (TSP-A, TSP-B, CA-P) all consistently model 208V TPN; SLD adopts the 208Y/120V building-service voltage from the project brief and the sub-panel intents. Upstream MSP-A panelboard intent should be re-authored to 208Y/120V 3-phase for consistency; flagged for db-layout follow-up.

Selectivity verified by manual_review for T01/T02 (200A/100A = 2:1 below 3:1 typical) and iec_60898_typical for T03 (200A/60A = 3.33:1). NEC does NOT mandate full selective coordination for general retail — only for elevators (NEC 620.62), emergency systems (NEC 700.27/701.27), and healthcare critical branches (NEC 517.17). Series-rated combinations per NEC 240.86 cover the partial-selectivity cases; AIC stacking confirmation by manufacturer table.

SPD Type 1+2 selected per NEC 285. Type 1 at service-entry (MSP-A intake, ahead of main disconnect per NEC 285.23) handles direct-strike via the utility service drop; Type 2 at the branch panels (NEC 285.24) handles residual transients. Combined Type 1+2 assembly at MSP-A is the standard US commercial practice.

Peak PFC 21.0 kA at MSP-A busbar is slightly under the declared 22.0 kA — accounting for the service-entrance conductor impedance between the utility transformer secondary and the MSP-A bus. Sub-panel PFCs are lower (TSP-A/TSP-B ~15-18 kA after 30-40m feeder run; CA-P ~12-14 kA after 50-60m to common-area closet). All within sub-panel AIC ratings (TSP-A/TSP-B 22 kA, CA-P 10 kA — CA-P feeder length helps drop PFC to within 10 kA rating).

Life-safety isolation at this scale: no dedicated fire-alarm panel (fire-alarm system on local 24V supervised loop with battery backup per NFPA 72), no central inverter emergency-lighting (per-fixture battery packs per NFPA 101). The CA-P C03 emergency-lighting circuit is a sustained-supply distribution feeder to the battery-pack luminaires — NOT a dedicated essential-loads bus.

drawing_notes: MSP-A located in service-entry room on north exterior wall with 200A 208Y/120V TPN intake from US utility TN-S supply.
3 tenant/common feeders from MSP-A: T01 (Suite 100 100A) + T02 (Suite 200 100A) + T03 (common area 60A).
Combined Type 1+2 SPD assembly at MSP-A intake per NEC 2023 Article 285.
Branch Type 2 SPDs at TSP-A, TSP-B, CA-P per NEC 285.24.
Cable conductors per upstream db-layout intents use AWG sizing (Cu, THHN insulation typical for US commercial sub-panel feeders).

CRITICAL: CA-P C05 fire-pump branch violates NEC 695.4(A) — fire-pump requires independent source. Relocate to dedicated FP-1 controller ahead of MSP-A main disconnect or to independent service drop per NEC 695.3.
flags: TOOL-CALL-PENDING:sld_system_metrics — System metrics are LLM-estimates; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.
NON-COMPLIANCE:critical — NEC 695.4(A) fire-pump on shared common-area panel (deliberate teaching scenario; see compliance_summary)

rationale.chat_summary: US strip-mall retail building on TN-S 208Y/120V 3-phase (Ze=0.10Ω, PFC=22kA). 200A MSP-A feeds 3 sub-panels: TSP-A (Suite 100 apparel 100A), TSP-B (Suite 200 food-service 100A), CA-P (common area + exterior 60A). Imax_total ~127A; peak PFC 21.0kA. Combined Type 1+2 SPD per NEC 285. Cascade is partially_selective overall (T01/T02 = 2:1 partial, T03 = 3.33:1 selective). CRITICAL non-compliance: CA-P C05 fire-pump violates NEC 695.4(A) independent-source rule — deliberate teaching scenario surfaced at SLD tier. Reply to refine.

sections.title: Supply Origin
sections.summary: TN-S 208Y/120V 3-phase 4-wire, declared Ze=0.10Ω + PFC=22kA. 200A panelboard main switch with 25kA AIC rating (per NEC 110.9 interrupting-rating). Generic US IOU service — citations use NEC 2023 form.

decisions.label: TN-S 208Y/120V service confirmed
decisions.summary: Separate neutral conductor from utility transformer secondary; service equipment grounding electrode conductor (GEC) per NEC 250.66 sized to service conductor ampacity.
decisions.rule: NEC 2023 Article 250 grounding/bonding for service equipment

sections.title: Distribution Hierarchy
sections.summary: 4-board cascade. MSP-A in service-entry room feeds 2 tenant sub-panels (TSP-A apparel + TSP-B food-service) and 1 common-area sub-panel (CA-P with parking-lot + exterior + fire-pump). Strip-mall topology typical for US small commercial.

decisions.label: Tenant-isolated sub-panels
decisions.summary: Each tenant gets a dedicated 100A 2-pole feeder for billing isolation + fit-out independence. CA-P handles landlord-side loads (common-area lighting, parking-lot, signage, fire-pump).
decisions.rule: Industry convention; NEC 230.71 disconnecting means per tenant

sections.title: System Metrics
sections.summary: Imax_total 127A (vs 200A intake = 64% utilisation, 36% headroom). Peak PFC 21.0kA (vs 25kA AIC — tight but acceptable, typical US commercial AIC margin). Sub-panel feeder runs drop PFC further at the branch tier.

decisions.label: Intake capacity verified
decisions.summary: main_switch_rating 200A >= imax_total 127A; AIC 25kA >= peak_pfc 21kA. NEC 110.9 interrupting rating satisfied.
decisions.rule: NEC 110.9 interrupting rating + NEC 220.10 calculated load

sections.title: Cascade Selectivity
sections.summary: T01/T02 (200A/100A = 2:1) flagged 'partial_selective' — below 3:1 typical but acceptable for general retail per NEC 240.86 series-rated combinations. T03 (200A/60A = 3.33:1) verified selective. Full selective coordination NOT required for general commercial per NEC scope — only elevators, emergency, healthcare critical.

decisions.label: Partial selectivity acceptable for general retail
decisions.summary: NEC does not mandate selective coordination for general commercial sub-panels. Series-rated AIC stacking per NEC 240.86 covers the partial-selectivity cases; manufacturer table consultation confirms specific breaker pair behaviour.
decisions.rule: NEC 240.86 series-rated combinations + NEC 620.62 / 700.27 selectivity scope (excluded)

sections.title: SPD Assessment
sections.summary: Combined Type 1+2 SPD at MSP-A intake per NEC 285. Type 1 at service-entry (ahead of main disconnect per NEC 285.23) handles direct-strike; Type 2 at branch panels per NEC 285.24 handles residual transients. Standard US commercial practice for strip-mall with rooftop exposure.

decisions.label: Type 1+2 SPD at MSP-A
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Article 285 service-entry SPD policy. Combined assembly preferred over separate Type 1 + Type 2 devices for installation simplicity at this scale.
decisions.rule: NEC 285 SPD installation requirements + UL 1449 SPD listing

sections.title: Life-Safety Isolation
sections.summary: No dedicated fire-alarm panel (fire-alarm on 24V supervised loop with NFPA 72 battery backup). No central inverter emergency-lighting (per-fixture battery packs per NFPA 101). CA-P C03 sustained-supply emergency-lighting feeder distributes to battery-pack luminaires. UPS-essential loads kVA = 0 at this scale.

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Non-compliant: CRITICAL flag on CA-P C05 fire-pump branch per NEC 695.4(A) — fire-pump must have independent source, NOT share a panel with common-area / parking-lot loads. Deliberate teaching scenario at this SLD tier showing how multi-board IR surfaces compliance tensions that single-board db-layout cannot.

decisions.label: NEC 695.4(A) critical non-compliance
decisions.summary: CA-P C05 60A Type D fire-pump branch on common-area panel — a fault elsewhere on CA-P could disable the fire-pump during a fire event. Remediation: dedicated 'FP-1' fire-pump controller fed directly from MSP-A line-side (ahead of main disconnect) or independent service drop per NEC 695.3.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: 6 assumptions captured covering diversity factor, MSP-A upstream voltage-arrangement mismatch (db-layout intent shows 240V single-phase split; SLD applies 208Y/120V per task brief), NEC selectivity-scope rationale, Type 1+2 SPD per NEC 285, PFC service-entrance correction, and life-safety strategy.
